evt_opec_uae_001.headline: UAE quits OPEC+ — crude oil prices spike as supply outlook worsens
evt_opec_uae_001.summary: The UAE has announced its departure from OPEC+, triggering an immediate spike in crude oil prices. UK businesses that purchase oil, fuel or petrochemicals from overseas suppliers denominated in USD now face higher costs on every transaction.
evt_opec_uae_001.fx_payment_logic: UK fuel importers and petrochemical buyers purchase in USD — an oil price spike means their next USD invoice is more expensive in GBP terms.
affected_sectors: UK fuel and petroleum importers paying USD
UK chemical importers with USD-denominated supplier invoices
UK manufacturers buying USD-priced raw materials
evt_opec_uae_001.sales_angle: Oil just spiked — UK businesses paying USD for fuel or chemicals are seeing their next invoice cost more in GBP. Lock in a rate now before it moves further.
who_pays_fx: UK importers buying petroleum products in USD
UK chemical distributors with USD supplier invoices
who_benefits: UK oil producers and North Sea operators

evt_gbp_record_short_002.headline: Institutional investors push GBP short positions to near-record — pound expected to weaken further
evt_gbp_record_short_002.summary: Asset managers have increased GBP short positions to 110,000 contracts, near an all-time record. This signals strong institutional conviction that sterling will fall. UK businesses paying European suppliers in EUR will see every invoice cost more as the pound weakens.
evt_gbp_record_short_002.fx_payment_logic: UK businesses paying EUR supplier invoices — wine merchants buying from French châteaux, food importers sourcing from Italian producers, furniture buyers paying German manufacturers — face rising GBP costs as sterling weakens.
affected_sectors: UK wine importers paying EUR to European producers
UK food importers paying EUR to European suppliers
UK furniture importers with EUR supplier invoices
evt_gbp_record_short_002.sales_angle: GBP is heading lower — UK businesses paying European suppliers in EUR are about to see every invoice cost more. Forward contract now locks in today's rate.
who_pays_fx: UK wine importers paying EUR to French and Italian producers
UK food importers sourcing from Europe
UK furniture and homeware importers with EUR invoices
who_benefits: UK exporters billing European customers in EUR

evt_fed_boe_003.headline: Fed and BoE both announce rate decisions this week — maximum GBP/USD volatility expected
evt_fed_boe_003.summary: Both the Federal Reserve and Bank of England announce rate decisions within days of each other. GBP/USD is already volatile and any surprise will move rates sharply. UK businesses with upcoming USD payments — importers, overseas buyers, businesses with US suppliers — face real uncertainty.
evt_fed_boe_003.fx_payment_logic: Any UK business making a USD payment this week faces maximum uncertainty — the rate they get on Thursday could be meaningfully worse than Monday if either central bank surprises markets.
affected_sectors: UK importers with upcoming USD payments
UK businesses sourcing from US suppliers
UK technology buyers paying USD SaaS subscriptions
UK seafood importers paying USD to Icelandic or Norwegian suppliers
evt_fed_boe_003.sales_angle: Both the Fed and BoE decide rates this week — GBP/USD will move sharply on any surprise. If you have a USD payment due, locking in now avoids the volatility.
who_pays_fx: UK importers with USD payments due this week
UK businesses with US dollar supplier invoices
any UK company with upcoming international payments
who_benefits: UK USD earners and exporters to the US

evt_iran_usd_004.headline: US-Iran tensions escalate — USD strengthens as safe-haven demand rises
evt_iran_usd_004.summary: Escalating US-Iran tensions are driving safe-haven demand for USD, pushing GBP/USD lower. UK businesses that pay USD-denominated invoices — fuel buyers, chemical importers, food manufacturers sourcing from the Americas — face higher costs in GBP for every transaction.
evt_iran_usd_004.fx_payment_logic: When USD strengthens against GBP, every UK business paying a USD invoice needs more pounds to cover the same cost — this is direct margin erosion for importers.
affected_sectors: UK food manufacturers sourcing USD-priced commodities
UK pharmaceutical importers with USD supplier invoices
evt_iran_usd_004.sales_angle: USD is strengthening on Iran tensions — UK businesses paying USD supplier invoices are losing margin on every transaction right now.
who_pays_fx: UK food manufacturers buying USD-priced commodities
UK pharmaceutical companies with US supplier payments

evt_tariff_whisky_005.headline: US Section 338 tariff risk remains — UK exporters to America face ongoing pricing uncertainty
evt_tariff_whisky_005.summary: The threat of new US Section 338 tariffs on UK exports creates ongoing uncertainty for UK businesses selling to American customers. UK exporters billing in USD face both tariff risk and currency risk — if the USD weakens or tariffs land, their margin on US sales is squeezed from both sides.
evt_tariff_whisky_005.fx_payment_logic: UK exporters billing US customers in USD receive fewer pounds when they convert their USD revenue — tariff uncertainty compounds the FX risk on their US income.
affected_sectors: UK Scotch whisky exporters receiving USD from US sales
UK pharmaceutical exporters with USD revenue
UK food and drink exporters billing US customers in USD
evt_tariff_whisky_005.sales_angle: US tariff uncertainty is real and GBP/USD is moving — UK exporters with USD revenue should review how they're converting their US income before the rate moves against them.
who_pays_fx: UK exporters receiving USD revenue from US customers
UK Scotch whisky producers with US sales
who_benefits: UK importers of US goods if tariffs reduce trade
